Let's face it, everybody's got two cents and everybody's got two minutes. So here's my two cents in under two minutes. Hey, look with Ron Paul, at least you know for sure what you're getting. So check this out. During a debate in September, Wolf Blitzer, we call Blitz now because Herman Cain blitzed him, asked him whether he thought what Wolf Blitzer said to him, do you think a sick and uninsured person should just be left to die? And Ron Paul says in Ron Paul fashion, without getting excited. He's not all grant rant, right? That's what freedom is all about, taking your own risk. That's conviction. You see, this guy's got conviction. He's got commitment. He's not flip-flopping. No double speak, no compromise. Hey, and I'm going to tell you something else I love about Ron Paul, okay? The guy wants to ban this mandatory child screening for mental health issues. Unbelievable that nobody else has stepped up to do that. Can you imagine being forced in America to screen your child for mental health problems? I remember me and my wife were having a baby and we found out she was pregnant. We went to the doctor. He's like, hey man, well you want to check for any problems? You know we do this test or that test. I forget what they call it. I'm sure many of you know what it is. I'm like, are we doing that test? Dude, I'm not getting rid of the baby no matter what, okay? You don't need to do the test. It's our baby. We conceived it. We're keeping it. It doesn't matter. So Ron Paul, man, I congratulate you for your stance on no screening, banning the screening of children for mental health because you know that's just a scam to put more kids on drugs and I also commend you, Ron Paul, for your persistence, your tenacity, for staying with it. Dude, for you being straight on, for you running by the Constitution, okay? For you being willing to say federal reserves a jack job, let's get back to the gold standard. Let's get back to not giving money to politicians that just blow it. Let's get back on budget. Let's make America strong. Hey, I'm going to tell you what I'd do. I'd put a moratorium on war. So I'd be like until we're in financially solvent, we're not doing war anywhere. Hey, once we're solvent again, maybe we go back and look at our war position and just be a defensive mechanism worldwide. Anyway, Ron Paul, I congratulate you, man. I really, really appreciate who you are and the kind of courage you have and other Americans, whether you vote for or not, should at least salute that he is a true American.
